mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 8% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal accommodative monetary policy and stable price expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to produce positive returns in 75%+ of triggered periods (p > 0.05 in t-test).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer credit availability (RSAFS) during periods of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) signals overleveraged households and potential monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of high-inflation periods despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window for INDPRO-based signals.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with rising HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 130,000 signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of periods with PAYEMS growth above threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based economic expansion that drives corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect fails to produce positive returns in 75%+ of cases (p > 0.05 in t-test).
